title: Mutton Karahi

seo.title: How to Make Mutton Karahi — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Mutton Karahi. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Mutton Karahi — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Mutton Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, known for its rich, aromatic gravy and tender pieces of mutton. Cooked in a traditional karahi (wok), it features a vibrant blend of fresh tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and an array of fragrant spices. This flavorful curry is a beloved staple, perfect for family dinners and special occasions alike.

instructions.title: Sear the Mutton
instructions.text: Heat ghee or oil in a karahi or heavy-bottomed pot over high heat. Add mutton pieces and sear until they change color and are lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes. This step helps lock in the juices.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the paste.

instructions.title: Incorporate Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Add chopped tomatoes, salt, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Mix well, cover, and cook on medium-low heat for 15-20 minutes, or until tomatoes soften and release their water, and the oil separates. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Tenderize with Yogurt
instructions.text: Add whisked yogurt and slit green chilies. Increase heat to medium-high and cook, stirring continuously, until the yogurt blends in and the gravy thickens slightly, about 5-7 minutes. Ensure no lumps form by stirring constantly.

instructions.title: Simmer to Perfection
instructions.text: Reduce heat to low, cover the karahi, and let it simmer for another 10-15 minutes, or until the mutton is fork-tender and the oil rises to the surface. If needed, add a splash of water to adjust gravy consistency to your liking.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in black pepper powder and garam masala. Garnish generously with julienned fresh ginger and chopped fresh coriander. Serve hot with naan, roti, or plain rice for a complete Pakistani meal.

tips: Always cook karahi on high heat initially to sear the meat, then reduce to medium-low to allow the tomatoes to break down completely and release their flavors. This slow cooking ensures a rich, smooth gravy.
For a truly authentic taste, use bone-in mutton as the bones add immense flavor to the curry. If mutton is unavailable, lamb or beef can be substituted, though cooking times may vary.
Mutton Karahi tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld together. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop.
article: Mutton Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
